name: Blockscout MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from Blockscout (blockscout org) for Blockscout — the open-source blockchain explorer for EVM-compatible chains. Provides AI agents contextual access to blockchain data including balances, transactions, tokens, smart contracts, and on-chain analytics across Ethereum and other EVM chains where Blockscout is deployed.
use_cases: Querying wallet balances and transaction history from DeFi analysis agents
Looking up smart contract details and interactions from development agents
Monitoring on-chain token transfers and events from blockchain analytics agents
Retrieving block and transaction data for blockchain audit agents
not_for: Teams not working with EVM-compatible blockchains
Real-time trading signals (Blockscout is explorer data, not market data)
Bitcoin or non-EVM chain data (Ethereum/EVM focus)
best_when: A development or analytics team working with EVM blockchains wants AI agents to query on-chain data — transactions, balances, contracts, tokens — through Blockscout's explorer API.
avoid_when: You need real-time price feeds or trading data (use dedicated crypto data APIs). Also note: crypto/blockchain context carries inherent financial risk.

auth.notes: Public Blockscout instances often require no auth. Self-hosted instances may require API keys. Auth depends on the specific Blockscout deployment.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Blockscout is open source — public instances are free to query
pricing.paid_tiers: Self-hosted or enterprise instances may have costs
pricing.notes: Blockscout is open source (APACHE). Public instances like Ethereum mainnet explorer are free. MCP server is free open source from blockscout org.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by Blockscout instance; public instances have informal rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Blockchain data — agents interacting with wallets or contracts must handle crypto assets carefully
Rate limits on public instances are undocumented — aggressive agents may get blocked
Data availability depends on which Blockscout instance is configured — mainnet vs testnets vary
Official from blockscout org — quality implementation but blockchain explorer APIs can be complex
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Blockchain/crypto context. Read-only explorer access. HTTPS to Blockscout instances. No private key handling in this server — data query only.

security_score_components.security_notes: Read-only blockchain data queries. No key/wallet management. HTTPS. Official open-source implementation.
